items.name: twoFactor
items.description: Zero-dependency Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) module. Implements TOTP (RFC 6238 / RFC 4226), backup codes, and composable middleware for step-up verification. Uses only Node.js built-in `crypto` — no external packages.

examples.title: Setup 2FA for a user
Verify a TOTP code

examples.code: app.use('/admin', twoFactor.require2FA(), adminRouter);
examples.title: Protect routes with 2FA middleware
Generate and redeem backup codes

methods.description: Get the last-used counter for a user.
Store the last-used counter for a user with a TTL.

methodParams.notes: The time-step counter that was used.

methods.description: Clear all stored counters (for testing or user revocation).
Destroy the store and stop periodic pruning.
Generate a cryptographically random TOTP secret.

methodParams.notes: Number of random bytes (default 20 = 160-bit, per RFC 4226).

methodReturns.type: { raw: Buffer, base32: string, hex: string }

methods.description: Generate a TOTP code for the current (or given) time.

methodOptions.notes: Unix timestamp in seconds (defaults to now).

examples.code:
const code = generateTOTP('JBSWY3DPEHPK3PXP');
  // '482913'
const code = generateTOTP(secret, { period: 60, algorithm: 'sha256', digits: 8 });
examples.title: Custom period and algorithm

methods.description: Verify a user-supplied TOTP code against a shared secret. Checks within a configurable time-step window to handle clock drift.

methodParams.notes: The 6/8-digit code submitted by the user.

methodOptions.notes: Number of periods to check before/after current.

methods.example:
const result = verifyTOTP('482913', userSecret);
  if (result.valid) console.log('Authenticated!', result.delta);

examples.code:
const result = verifyTOTP('482913', userSecret);
  if (result.valid) console.log('Authenticated!', result.delta);
const result = verifyTOTP(code, secret, { window: 2 });
examples.title: Wider window for unreliable clocks

methods.description: Generate an `otpauth://` URI for QR code enrollment. Compatible with Google Authenticator, Authy, 1Password, etc.
Generate a set of single-use backup/recovery codes. Returns both the plaintext codes (show once to user) and SHA-256 hashes (store in database).

methodParams.notes: Random bytes per code (4 bytes → 8 hex chars).

methods.description: Verify a backup code against stored hashes. On match, returns the index so the caller can remove/mark it as used.

methodParams.notes: Array of SHA-256 hex hashes stored in DB.

methods.description: Middleware that requires completed 2FA verification on the session. Checks `req.session.get('twoFactorVerified')` — returns 403 if not set. Designed to compose with `jwt()` or `session()` middleware: app.use(jwt({ secret })); app.use(require2FA()); // — or — app.use(session({ secret })); app.use(require2FA());

methodOptions.notes:
`(req) => boolean|Promise<boolean>`.
Return `false` to skip the 2FA check for users who haven't enrolled.
Defaults to always requiring 2FA.

examples.code: app.use(require2FA());
examples.title: Basic — all authenticated users must complete 2FA
Only enforce for users who have enrolled

examples.code: app.use(require2FA({ sessionKey: 'mfaComplete' }));
examples.title: Custom session key

methods.description: Rate-limited TOTP verification middleware. Wraps `verifyTOTP` with attempt tracking to prevent brute-force attacks. Tracks attempts per-IP in memory with automatic expiry. Supports optional replay prevention (RFC 6238 §5.2) via a `replayStore`. When configured, each successfully verified TOTP counter is recorded and rejected on subsequent use within the validity window.

methodOptions.notes:
`(req) => string|Buffer|Promise<string|Buffer>`.
Retrieves the user's TOTP secret from your database.
Request body field containing the code.
Session key to set on success.

Store for TOTP replay prevention.
Must implement `get(userId): Promise<number|null>` and
`set(userId, counter, ttlMs): Promise<void>`.

`(req) => string|Promise<string>`.
Required when `replayStore` is set. Returns a unique user identifier.
`(req, res, attemptsLeft) => void` called on failure.

examples.title: With replay prevention

methods.description: Combined verification middleware that auto-detects and handles TOTP codes, backup codes, or WebAuthn/passkey assertions from a single endpoint. Detection logic (based on request body shape): - `{ code: "123456" }` → TOTP verification - `{ backupCode: "a1b2c3d4" }` → Backup code redemption - `{ id, response: { authenticatorData, clientDataJSON, signature } }` → WebAuthn assertion Rate-limits across all methods using a shared per-IP attempt tracker.

methodOptions.notes:
`(req, index) => void|Promise<void>`.
Called when a backup code is redeemed so the caller can remove it.

`(req, credId) => object|Promise<object>`.
Retrieves a stored WebAuthn credential by ID. Return `{ publicKey, counter, ... }`.

`(req, credId, newCounter) => void|Promise<void>`.
Persist the updated signature counter after WebAuthn verification.
Expected relying party ID for WebAuthn.
Session key to set on success.
`(req, res, method) => void`. Called on success with the method used.
`(req, res, attemptsLeft) => void`. Called on failure.

items.name: webauthn
items.description: Zero-dependency WebAuthn/FIDO2/Passkeys implementation. Supports registration (attestation) and authentication (assertion) ceremonies using only Node.js built-in `crypto`. Implements: - Challenge generation (cryptographically random, ≥16 bytes) - Registration options & verification (none, packed, fido-u2f attestation) - Authentication options & verification with counter validation - CBOR decoding for attestation objects and authenticator data - COSE key parsing (EC2/P-256, RSA, OKP/Ed25519) - ES256, RS256, EdDSA signature verification

examples.title: Registration
Authentication

items.name: trustedDevice
items.description: Trusted Device / "Remember Me" middleware for 2FA. After successful 2FA verification, issues an encrypted device-trust token stored as an HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Strict cookie. Subsequent requests skip the 2FA prompt if the trust token is valid. Supports secret rotation, IP binding, and revocation. Uses AES-256-GCM encryption — tokens are encrypted, not just signed, preventing information leakage.

options.notes:
`(req) => string` device fingerprint.
Defaults to User-Agent hash.

`(req) => string` user identifier.
Defaults to `req.user.id || req.user.sub`.

items.name: enrollment
items.description: 2FA Enrollment Flow Helper. Provides a session-scoped, multi-step enrollment workflow for TOTP-based two-factor authentication. Steps: 1. `start()` — Generate secret + backup codes, store in session 2. `verify()` — Confirm user can produce a valid TOTP code 3. `complete()` — Persist the verified secret to the database 4. `disable()` — Remove 2FA from the account

options.notes:
`(req, base32Secret, backupHashes) => Promise<void>`.
Persist the verified TOTP secret and backup hashes.

`(req) => Promise<void>`.
Remove TOTP secret on disable.
Issuer name for the otpauth URI.

`(req) => string`. User label for QR code.
Defaults to `req.user.email || req.user.id`.
Enrollment session TTL in ms (default 10 min).
Number of backup codes to generate.

`(req) => boolean|Promise<boolean>`.
Check if 2FA is already enabled (for guarding start/disable).

returns.type: { start: Function, verify: Function, complete: Function, disable: Function }

examples.title: Full enrollment flow
